mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate profit growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real private credit and a decline in housing starts indicates credit reallocation away from productive investment toward speculative real estate, which destabilizes financial conditions and yields negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample quarterly return regressions over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month industrial production growth during a late-cycle expansion reflects resilient manufacturing output that supports near-term earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months with positive INDPRO growth during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses real corporate margins and triggers risk-off equity positioning, resulting in negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered instances across a 4-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly return direction aligns with GDPC1 growth in fewer than 50 percent of observations over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200 thousand additions in a single month confirms sustained labor market tightness that supports wage growth and consumer expenditure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of months where PAYEMS growth surpasses 200 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during a monetary easing cycle signal renewed residential investment demand that boosts construction sector earnings and broader economic sentiment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with the HOUST-FEDFUNDS interaction term (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.
